items.question: "I'm hungry." → She said she ___ hungry.
explanations: After 'said', present simple backshifts to past simple.
Correct! Present simple 'am' → past simple 'was': 'She said she was hungry'.
'Will be' changes the meaning to the future.
'Has been' isn't the backshift of 'am'.

items.question: "I work in a bank." → He said he ___ in a bank.
explanations: 'Works' keeps the present after a past reporting verb.
Correct! Present simple → past simple: 'He said he worked in a bank'.
'Had worked' is the backshift of the past simple, not the present.
'Would work' is the backshift of 'will work'.

items.question: "I've finished the report." → She said she ___ the report.
explanations: 'Finished' is possible informally, but the standard backshift is the past perfect.
'Has finished' keeps the present perfect, which should backshift.
Correct! Present perfect → past perfect: 'She said she had finished the report'.
'Would finish' is the backshift of 'will finish'.

items.question: "I will call you." → He said he ___ call me.
explanations: 'Will' should backshift after a past reporting verb.
Correct! 'Will' → 'would': 'He said he would call me'.
'Wills' is not a word.
'Had call' is not correct English.

items.question: "I can swim." → She said she ___ swim.
explanations: 'Can' should backshift after 'said'.
Correct! 'Can' → 'could': 'She said she could swim'.
Never add 'to' after a modal.
'Would can' is not correct English.

items.question: "I like your jacket." → He said he liked ___ jacket.
explanations: 'Your' refers to the original listener; the pronoun must shift.
Correct! Speaking to me, 'your' becomes 'my': 'He said he liked my jacket'.
'His' would mean a third person's jacket.
'Their' refers to other people.

items.question: "I'll see you tomorrow." (said on Monday) → She said she'd see me ___.
explanations: 'Tomorrow' shifts when we report later.
Correct! 'Tomorrow' → 'the next day': 'She said she'd see me the next day'.
'Yesterday' points to the past, changing the meaning.
'Now' doesn't fit a future arrangement.

items.question: Correct this: 'She said she is coming later.'
options: She said she was coming later.
She said she is coming later.
She said that is she coming later.
explanations: Correct! After 'said', 'is coming' backshifts to 'was coming'.
'Is coming' should backshift after a past reporting verb.
'She come' is grammatically wrong.
This uses question word order, which is wrong for a statement.

items.question: "We are moving house." → They said they ___ house.
explanations: 'Are moving' should backshift after a past reporting verb.
Correct! Present continuous → past continuous: 'They said they were moving house'.
'Had moved' is the backshift of a completed action, not 'are moving'.
'Would move' is the backshift of 'will move'.

items.question: Which sentence does NOT need backshift?
options: "Water boils at 100°C." → She said water ___ at 100°C.
explanations: A past statement still backshifts (to the past perfect) in careful English.
Correct! A general truth can stay in the present: 'She said water boils at 100°C'.
'I am tired' backshifts to 'he was tired'.
'I will help' backshifts to 'she would help'.
